trails.name: Alta Via 1 - Classic Dolomites Trek
trails.description: The most famous multi-day trek in the Dolomites, traversing stunning mountain landscapes from north to south through the heart of the Dolomites UNESCO World Heritage site.
highlights: Traverse the legendary Dolomites peaks
Stay in authentic mountain rifugios
Experience sunrise and sunset at high altitude
Cross dramatic mountain passes
Immerse in alpine culture and cuisine

stages.name: Lago di Braies to Rifugio Sennes
stages.description: Begin your Dolomites adventure from the iconic Lago di Braies, climbing through alpine meadows and past the charming Malga Fodara Vedla to reach Rifugio Sennes with sweeping panoramic views over the Fanes plateau.
highlights: Stunning views of Lago di Braies
Alpine meadows filled with wildflowers
First glimpse of the Sennes plateau

stops.name: Malga Fodara Vedla
stops.what: Cold drinks, local cheese, Speck Alto Adige, homemade bread

sleep.booking_note: Book 3–4 weeks ahead in high season. Call +39 0474 501057 — phone preferred over email.

exit_routes.name: Descent to Pederü and San Vigilio di Marebbe
exit_routes.description: From Malga Fodara Vedla, follow the signed jeep track west down to Pederü (1,548m). From Pederü, a private road leads to San Vigilio di Marebbe (Enneberg). Do not attempt the descent in heavy rain or fog — the path becomes slippery.
exit_routes.transport: SAD bus line 442 from San Vigilio di Marebbe to Bruneck/Brunico (hourly in summer). Taxi available: +39 0474 501014.

rejoining_options.description: Return to Rifugio Sennes the next day
rejoining_options.how: Take the Pederü jeep road back up to Malga Fodara Vedla and continue on trail 7 to Rifugio Sennes. Allow 3.5 hours.

rejoining_options.description: Skip to Rifugio Fanes and join Stage 2
rejoining_options.how: From Pederü, take the trail to Rifugio Fanes directly via Val di Fanes (trail 11, approx 3.5 hours). This merges you into Stage 2.

stages.weather_risk: Mostly forested and below the treeline — lower risk than later stages. Check the morning forecast. Afternoon storms are common from June to August; aim to reach Rifugio Sennes before 14:00.

stages.name: Rifugio Sennes to Rifugio Fanes
stages.description: Traverse the open Sennes plateau with sweeping Dolomite views, cross the high Passo Sennes, and descend to the emerald Lago di Fanes before arriving at the beautifully situated Rifugio Fanes.
highlights: Crossing the high Passo Sennes at 2,424m
Stunning emerald Lago di Fanes
Wildlife spotting — ibex and golden eagles

stops.name: Lago di Fanes — Malga Fanes Piccola
stops.what: Hot drinks, cold drinks, cheese plate, seasonal soups

sleep.booking_note: Book 3–4 weeks ahead. Call +39 0474 501097. Rifugio fills quickly in July–August — evening meals are served at set times, confirm on arrival.

exit_routes.name: Descent to Armentarola via Val Medesc
exit_routes.description: From near Lago di Fanes, follow trail 10 southwest through Val Medesc, descending to Armentarola (1,643m) at the head of Val Badia. Well-marked; allow 1.5 hours. In poor weather, this is the safest and quickest exit on this stage.
exit_routes.transport: SAD bus line 040 from Armentarola to Bruneck/Brunico and La Villa. Also taxis from San Cassiano (+39 0471 849503).

rejoining_options.description: Return to Rifugio Fanes and continue
rejoining_options.how: From Armentarola, take trail 10 back up Val Medesc to Rifugio Fanes (1.5 hours). Rest and continue Stage 3 the next morning.

rejoining_options.description: Skip Stage 3 and join at Rifugio Lavarella
rejoining_options.how: From Armentarola, follow trail 11 up through Val di Fanes to Rifugio Lavarella (approx 3 hours). This rejoins the route at the start of Stage 4.

stages.weather_risk: Passo Sennes (2,424m) is fully exposed — lightning risk in afternoon storms. Leave Rifugio Sennes by 07:30 to cross the pass before midday. If clouds build rapidly, turn back and take the Val Medesc exit to Armentarola.

stages.name: Rifugio Fanes to Rifugio Lavarella
stages.description: An easier day through the beautiful Val di Fanes, following streams and alpine pastures rich with wildflowers and marmots. A perfect rest-day stage to let your legs recover before the challenging Stage 4.
highlights: Gentle valley walking through the Fanes plateau
Marmot colonies along the trail
Peaceful alpine streams and wildflowers

stops.name: Ütia de Börz (Alpe di Fanes malga)
stops.what: Cold drinks, local cheeses, Schlutzkrapfen (South Tyrolean dumplings), yogurt

sleep.booking_note: Book 3–4 weeks ahead. Call +39 0474 501159. Known for excellent South Tyrolean cooking — confirm dinner time on arrival.

exit_routes.name: Descent to Armentarola
exit_routes.description: From anywhere along Val di Fanes, follow the main valley floor trail north back to the Armentarola road junction, then descend to Armentarola (1,643m). The trail runs along the valley bottom — straightforward navigation even in mist.
exit_routes.transport: SAD bus line 040 from Armentarola to Bruneck/Brunico and La Villa. Taxi from San Cassiano (+39 0471 849503).

rejoining_options.description: Return to Rifugio Lavarella and continue
rejoining_options.how: From Armentarola, re-enter Val di Fanes on foot (approx 2 hours) or ask at the tourist office in San Cassiano about a private shuttle as far as the road allows.

rejoining_options.description: Skip to Rifugio Scotoni and join Stage 4
rejoining_options.how: From San Cassiano, take a taxi up the Armentarola road to Rifugio Scotoni (1,985m) — the road is accessible to private vehicles. Rejoins route at the start of Stage 5.

stages.weather_risk: Low risk — this stage stays mostly in the sheltered valley below 2,100m. However, lightning can still reach valley floors. If storms forecast, complete the stage early and shelter at Ütia de Börz until the cell passes.

stages.name: Rifugio Lavarella to Rifugio Scotoni
stages.description: The most demanding stage of the trek — a sustained climb to the dramatic Forcella Lavarella pass at 2,462m with breathtaking 360° views, followed by a long descent through the remote Vallon Bianco to Rifugio Scotoni beneath the towering Tofane massif.
highlights: Forcella Lavarella — dramatic high mountain pass at 2,462m
360° panoramic views of the Dolomiti Ampezzane
Views of the Tofane massif and Lagazuoi

stops.name: Rest point below Forcella Lavarella — no services
stops.what: No facilities — bring lunch from Rifugio Lavarella. Water from stream at the base of the forcella approach (treat if uncertain).

stops.name: Rifugio Scotoni bar — arrives at destination
stops.what: Hot meals, cold drinks, local specialties — reward yourself at the rifugio bar after this demanding stage.

sleep.booking_note: Book 4–6 weeks ahead in July–August. Call +39 0436 2939. Rifugio Scotoni is one of the most popular stops on the route — don't leave this to chance.

exit_routes.name: Retreat to Armentarola via Vallon Bianco descent
exit_routes.description: If conditions deteriorate on or before Forcella Lavarella, do NOT proceed over the pass. Retrace to Rifugio Lavarella (1 hour). From Lavarella, follow the Val di Fanes exit to Armentarola (see Stage 3 exit). If you have already crossed the forcella and entered Vallon Bianco, continue descending — the valley eventually leads to the Armentarola road.
exit_routes.transport: SAD bus line 040 from Armentarola to Bruneck/Brunico. Taxi from San Cassiano (+39 0471 849503). From Rifugio Scotoni, the Col Gallina road allows taxi access.

rejoining_options.description: Rest in Armentarola and attempt Stage 4 the next day
rejoining_options.how: Favourable weather windows sometimes open after a storm day. Take the Armentarola road to Rifugio Lavarella early the following morning and attempt the pass before noon.

rejoining_options.description: Skip to Rifugio Scotoni and join Stage 5
rejoining_options.how: From San Cassiano, take a taxi along the Col Gallina pass road directly to Rifugio Scotoni (road-accessible). This skips Stage 4 and joins Stage 5 at its start.

stages.weather_risk: HIGH RISK — Forcella Lavarella (2,462m) is fully exposed and the highest point of this itinerary. Strong winds can make the pass dangerous. Check the evening forecast at Rifugio Lavarella. Leave no later than 07:00 and aim to cross the forcella by 10:00. Turn back immediately if you see anvil-shaped clouds or hear distant thunder.

stages.name: Rifugio Scotoni to Cortina d'Ampezzo
stages.description: A rewarding final day descending beneath the iconic Tofane massif, through forests and open hillsides with views that make you glad you made it this far. Arrive in the elegant resort town of Cortina d'Ampezzo and celebrate your Alta Via 1 completion.
highlights: Views of the Tofane massif throughout the descent
Ra Valles — classic Dolomites viewpoint
Arriving in historic Cortina d'Ampezzo

stops.name: Ra Valles — gondola station bar
stops.what: Hot meals, cold drinks, espresso, fresh pastries. Last mountain stop before Cortina.

sleep.booking_note: Sleep in Cortina d'Ampezzo — hotels and B&Bs available at all price points. Book ahead in July–August when Cortina is busy.

exit_routes.name: Ra Valles gondola shortcut to Cortina
exit_routes.description: At Ra Valles gondola station (km 5.2), take the Freccia nel Cielo gondola directly down to Cortina d'Ampezzo if weather deteriorates or legs are tired. The gondola operates June–September. This is not an emergency exit — it simply shortens the final descent by about 1.5 hours.
exit_routes.transport: Freccia nel Cielo gondola (Ra Valles to Cortina — Rumerlo station). Approx €15 one-way. From Cortina, bus connections to Belluno, Venice, and Venice Airport.

rejoining_options.description: You have arrived — this is the final stage
rejoining_options.how: Cortina d'Ampezzo is the endpoint of this itinerary. Celebrate with an Aperol Spritz on the main Corso Italia. Well done.

stages.weather_risk: Lower risk — descending throughout. If morning storms forecast, delay start by 1–2 hours or take the Ra Valles gondola to avoid the exposed upper section. No significant exposed sections on this stage.

equipment_checklist: Sleeping bag liner (most rifugios provide blankets but not sheets)
Hiking boots with ankle support (break in before the trek)
Warm mid-layer (fleece or down jacket — temperatures above 2,000m drop fast)
Extra merino wool socks (at least 3 pairs)
Sunscreen SPF 50+ and sunglasses (UV intensity is high at altitude)
High-energy snacks and lunch for Stage 4 (no services on the pass)
booking_tips: Book all rifugios 4–6 weeks in advance in July–August — they fill completely
Call rather than email — rifugios in this area often have unreliable internet
Confirm bookings 48 hours before you set out each day
Half-board (dinner + breakfast) is expected and strongly recommended
Bring cash — Rifugio Sennes, Fanes, and Lavarella do not accept credit cards
Stage 4 has no services on the pass — prepare lunch the evening before at Rifugio Lavarella

josephineNote.en: This is one of the great Dolomite classics. Do it in late September when the crowds are gone and the larch trees are golden — the light filtering through the larches near Rifugio Fanes is something you will carry with you for years.
josephineNote.it: Questa è una delle grandi classiche delle Dolomiti. Fatela a fine settembre, quando le folle sono sparite e i larici sono dorati — la luce che filtra tra i larici vicino al Rifugio Fanes è qualcosa che vi porterete dentro per anni.
josephineNote.de: Das ist einer der großen Dolomiten-Klassiker. Geht ihn Ende September, wenn die Menschenmassen weg sind und die Lärchen golden leuchten — das Licht, das bei der Rifugio Fanes durch die Lärchen fällt, ist etwas, das ihr jahrelang mit euch tragen werdet.

trails.booking_strategy: Book all rifugios 4–6 weeks ahead in July–August. Most accept phone bookings; some have online booking via their websites. Cancel at least 48h ahead to free your bed for the next hiker.
